Author: Boujee Becky
Name: Deathroll Manager
Punchline: Track and run Deathroll games at FFXIV venues with live danger meters, battle animations, and tournament brackets.
Description: Track live Deathroll games with a real-time danger meter and animated stick-figure battle scene. Run full tournaments with single or double elimination brackets in V-bracket or left-to-right layouts. The relay system broadcasts your bracket to spectators via /say with a 6-character code and syncs live to a web viewer at tometools.com/bracket — no plugin required for spectators. Venue-tagged leaderboards track wins, losses, net gil, and win rates across all your events. Roll button auto-sends /random when it is your turn. Games and history persist between sessions.
Changelog: v1.2.5 — The Name Keeps Coming Back 👻 (not anymore!). Fixed: detected rolls no longer force-fill Player 1 every frame — backspace now sticks. New → P1 / → P2 / Dismiss buttons let you place a detected roll in the right slot or tell it to go away. Clear button wipes the setup form in one click (venue kept). Manual roll-off pick: if your client missed a /random 10, just click who won — no more waiting forever. Pre-roll ⇄ Swap button for when the order came out wrong. Auto-swap note so you know when the plugin quietly reordered things. My Stats now uses lenient name matching so first-name history entries count. Plus undo/redo correctly round-trips auto-swap state, and event handlers now clean up properly on reload.
